08/02/2026 - 13/02/2026

10/02/2026 09:57

Results:

Something is very wrong with channel 2. We'll inspect that. But first, some proof that channel 0 and 12 are statistcally different:

Paired \chi^2χ2\chi^2χ2 results (02-10-26: pairs with 0 vs pairs with 12)

It seems channel 2 is consistently not converging becuase midas consistently under-reports the number of events, so it never meets the threshold of seeing packet_rate > 1.99 * rate * packets_per_event. I'll try to rewire the scan so we don't rely on midas' event rate to see if this is a real effect or not.

10/02/2026 10:41

In regards to seeing a "~40ns resolution". This comes from the way the scan is done. There is a tolerance of 50ns. This effectively means the scan will always take 17 steps becuase our range is ~5,000,000 ns.

\text{Resolution} = \frac{\text{Range}}{2^{N_\text{steps}}} = \frac{5000000 - 500}{2^{17}} \approx 38.143158 \text{ ns} \approx 40 \text{ ns}
Resolution=Range2Nsteps=5000000−500217≈38.143158 ns≈40 ns\text{Resolution} = \frac{\text{Range}}{2^{N_\text{steps}}} = \frac{5000000 - 500}{2^{17}} \approx 38.143158 \text{ ns} \approx 40 \text{ ns}Resolution=2Nsteps​Range​=2175000000−500​≈38.143158 ns≈40 ns

So this is why we see that resolution.
